title: Transactional idempotency with artifact reconciliation
description: Make database and filesystem mutations safely repeatable for agents.
decision: Every public mutation requires an idempotency key. The identity is contract version plus operation ID plus key; comparison uses a canonical request hash including attributable actor context. Validation failures and busy/resource failures do not consume the key. Database effects and the stable structured result commit with the IdempotencyRecord in one transaction. Filesystem artifacts use a deterministic artifact ID, private staging path, checksum manifest, flush, verification, and atomic publication; retries reconcile a matching published artifact if publication preceded outcome recording.
rationale: Database uniqueness alone cannot make backup, restore, migration, or skill file effects exactly repeatable across crashes. Stable artifact identities and manifests bridge the database/filesystem durability boundary without distributed transactions.
alternatives: A: caller retries without persisted keys — rejected. B: database idempotency only — insufficient for files. C: transactional record plus artifact reconciliation — chosen.
consequences: A reused key with any changed semantic request returns conflict. Successful replay returns the original result without volatile fields. Operation descriptors declare whether a command is mutating and therefore requires a key.
status_notes: Accepted. Planned implementation areas: src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Contract and Recovery.
